name: jaz-ai
description: Jaz in your agents: full-featured accounting, finance, and reporting.
long_description: Built for accountants, finance teams, and the businesses they serve. IFRS-first, multi-currency, multi-user by default.

api_key.title: Jaz API Key or Access Token
api_key.description: Enter a Jaz API key or access token. Get one from jaz.ai > Settings.

tools.name: get_organization
tools.description: Get organization details: name, base currency, country, and financial year end.

tools.name: list_accounts
tools.description: List chart of accounts. Returns account name, code, type, class, status. Paginated — response includes totalElements. Use limit/offset to page.

tools.name: search_accounts
tools.description: Search chart of accounts. For OR (name or code), use filter: {"or":{"name":{"contains":"X"},"code":{"contains":"X"}}}.

tools.name: create_account
tools.description: Create a new chart of accounts entry. Auto-checks for duplicates by name — returns existing account if found. Code must be unique. Account class is inferred from accountType.

tools.name: update_account
tools.description: Update a chart of accounts entry — rename, re-code, set its period lock date, or remove an existing lock date. Setting lockDate (YYYY-MM-DD) is how an individual ledger account is locked for a period: it blocks recording or editing any transaction on that account dated on or before the lock date. Pass clearLockDate to remove (unlock) an existing lock.

tools.name: bulk_upsert_chart_of_accounts
tools.description:
Bulk create/update CoA entries (max 500). SYNC: returns { resourceIds, failedRows: [{rowIndex, columnName, columnValue, errorCode, errorMessage}], failedCount } — no jobId polling. PARTIAL_SUCCESS: failed rows surface in failedRows[]; others still succeed.

resourceId per row → update; omit → create. Note: dedup is by NAME (not code) — duplicate name surfaces ORGANIZATION_CHART_OF_ACCOUNT_DUPLICATED per row. Accepts classic 12 + 9 IFRS 18 accountType values; common variants normalized via normalizeAccountType.

Use for CoA imports / IFRS 18 migration / template seeding. For one-off creates with dedup, use create_account.

tools.name: list_contacts
tools.description: List contacts (customers/suppliers). Returns billingName, name, emails, status. Paginated — response includes totalElements. Use limit/offset to page.

tools.name: search_contacts
tools.description: Search contacts.

tools.name: get_contact
tools.description: Get full contact details by resourceId.

tools.name: get_contact_signals
tools.description:
Read-only contact pattern intelligence (cadence, outliers, divergences, severity). USE THIS for stand-alone history lookup before drafting (e.g. "what does this supplier normally look like?"). Use validate_drafts instead when you have a specific draft to score.

Returns null when the contact has no qualifying history yet (sampleSize < 5) or the freshness layer is unavailable. Per-pair cache (contactId × btType) — duplicate calls for the same pair return the same result.

Three response slices (severitySummary, outlierFlags, revealedDivergences) populate ONLY on the validate_drafts path — empty/null here.

tools.name: create_contact
tools.description: Create a new contact. Auto-checks for duplicates by name — returns existing contact if found instead of creating a duplicate.

tools.name: update_contact
tools.description: Update an existing contact. Fetches current data first so you only need to send changed fields.

tools.name: list_invoices
tools.description: List invoices. Returns reference, date, status, contact, totalAmount. Paginated — response includes totalElements. Use limit/offset to page.

tools.name: search_invoices
tools.description: Search invoices. Supports comma-separated status (e.g., "UNPAID,PARTIALLY_PAID"). Use filter param for advanced queries (nested paths, OR groups).

tools.name: get_invoice
tools.description: Get full invoice details including line items, payments, totals.

tools.name: create_invoice
tools.description:
Create invoice. lineItem text field = `name` (not "description"). saveAsDraft defaults true — false only on "finalize".

accountResourceId per lineItem required only when finalizing. lineItem minimum: `{ name, unitPrice, quantity, accountResourceId }`. Currency: `{ sourceCurrency: "USD", exchangeRate: 1.35 }`. Dates YYYY-MM-DD.

reference MUST be unique per org — auto-generate with timestamp if user doesn't specify (e.g. INV-20260309-1430). Duplicate → 422.

Optional capsuleRecipe (SALE base only — DEFERRED_REVENUE). Other recipes silently null here; pre-flight with preview_capsule_recipe. Mutex with capsuleResourceId. Rule 143.

tools.name: update_invoice
tools.description: Update an existing draft invoice (change reference, dates, line items, notes, custom fields). Use when the user says "update", "change", "fix", or "correct" a draft invoice. Line items CAN be fully replaced — pass the complete updated lineItems array. Optional capsuleRecipe retro-attaches a recipe on activation; same base-type constraint as create variant. Mutex with capsuleResourceId. Rule 143.

tools.name: delete_invoice
tools.description: Delete a draft invoice. Cannot delete finalized invoices.

tools.name: pay_invoice
tools.description: Record a payment against an invoice. Invoice must be APPROVED/UNPAID before payment — draft or voided invoices cannot be paid. If the invoice is still a DRAFT, call finalize_invoice first.

tools.name: finalize_invoice
tools.description: Finalize a draft invoice (set saveAsDraft=false). Can optionally update fields in the same call. IMPORTANT: Every lineItem MUST have accountResourceId. If the draft was created without it, pass lineItems with accountResourceId added (search accounts first, e.g. Operating Revenue for sales).

tools.name: apply_credits_to_invoice
tools.description:
Apply customer credit note(s) to an invoice. You MUST call this tool to actually apply credits — do not just describe the steps.
Steps: 1) search_customer_credit_notes with status UNAPPLIED for the same contact. 2) If CN is still DRAFT, call finalize_customer_credit_note first. 3) Call this tool with creditNoteResourceId and amountApplied for each CN.

tools.name: download_invoice_pdf
tools.description: Download an invoice as PDF. Returns { fileUrl }.

tools.name: list_bills
tools.description: List bills (purchase invoices). Returns reference, date, status, contact, amounts. Paginated — response includes totalElements. Use limit/offset to page.

tools.name: search_bills
tools.description: Search bills. Supports comma-separated status (e.g., "UNPAID,PARTIALLY_PAID"). Use filter param for advanced queries.

tools.name: get_bill
tools.description: Get full bill details.

tools.name: create_bill
tools.description: Create a new bill. Same rules as create_invoice: name not description, saveAsDraft defaults true, accountResourceId REQUIRED on lineItems for finalized bills (search accounts first, e.g. Operating Expense). When selecting a tax profile, use search_tax_profiles with appliesTo "purchase" — sales-only profiles cause 422. reference MUST be unique — generate one with a timestamp if user doesn't specify. Optional capsuleRecipe (PURCHASE base only — PREPAID_AMORTIZATION). Other recipes silently null here; pre-flight with preview_capsule_recipe. Mutex with capsuleResourceId. Rule 143.

tools.name: update_bill
tools.description: Update an existing draft bill (change reference, dates, line items, notes, custom fields). Use when the user says "update", "change", "fix", or "correct" a draft bill. Line items CAN be fully replaced — pass the complete updated lineItems array. Optional capsuleRecipe retro-attaches a recipe on activation; same base-type constraint as create variant. Mutex with capsuleResourceId. Rule 143.

tools.name: delete_bill
tools.description: Delete a draft bill.

tools.name: download_bill_pdf
tools.description: Download a bill as PDF. Returns { fileUrl } (may be pending/empty if the document is not yet rendered).

tools.name: pay_bill
tools.description: Record a payment against a bill. Bill must be APPROVED before payment — draft or voided bills cannot be paid.

tools.name: finalize_bill
tools.description: Finalize a draft bill (set saveAsDraft=false). Can optionally update fields in the same call. IMPORTANT: Every lineItem MUST have accountResourceId. If the draft was created without it, pass lineItems with accountResourceId added (search accounts first, e.g. Operating Expense for purchases).

tools.name: apply_credits_to_bill
tools.description: Apply supplier credit note(s) to a bill. IMPORTANT: The credit note must be FINALIZED first (status UNAPPLIED, not DRAFT). If it is still a draft, call finalize_supplier_credit_note first. Then search_supplier_credit_notes with status UNAPPLIED to find available credits for the contact, and pass creditNoteResourceId and amountApplied for each.

tools.name: create_sale_order
tools.description:
Create a Sale Quote or Sale Order (pick via documentType). lineItem text field = `name`. Dates YYYY-MM-DD. `reference` auto-generates (timestamped) if omitted — must be unique per org.

SALE_QUOTE: a draft estimate by default (saveAsDraft true → DRAFT). Lifecycle DRAFT → (issue: saveAsDraft:false) → CREATED → (transition_sale_order ACCEPT) → ACCEPTED. To order from a quote, create it with saveAsDraft:false — a DRAFT quote can be neither linked nor accepted.
SALE_ORDER: created directly as CREATED (no draft state). Link to a quote via saleQuoteResourceId — the quote MUST be CREATED or ACCEPTED (not DRAFT/VOID). Lifecycle CREATED → CONFIRMED (transition_sale_order CONFIRM).

accountResourceId is required on each lineItem when the document is not a draft. There is no order→invoice link field — raise the invoice separately with create_invoice.

tools.name: get_sale_order
tools.description: Get a Sale Quote or Sale Order by resourceId — line items, totals, status, orderState (fulfillment), and saleQuoteResourceId link.

tools.name: search_sale_orders
tools.description: Search Sale Quotes or Sale Orders (by documentType). Filter by status, contact, date, amount, currency, tag. Use the filter param for advanced/nested queries.

tools.name: update_sale_order
tools.description: Update an editable Sale Quote or Sale Order (reference, dates, line items, notes, custom fields). Line items are fully replaced — pass the complete array.

tools.name: transition_sale_order
tools.description:
Advance or cancel a Sale Quote / Sale Order lifecycle. action:
- ACCEPT — Sale Quote only (CREATED → ACCEPTED). The quote must already be issued (saveAsDraft:false); a DRAFT can't be accepted. A CREATED quote is already linkable, so accept is optional.
- CONFIRM — Sale Order only (CREATED → CONFIRMED).
- VOID — either, when not a draft (cancels the document).
- DELETE — DRAFT only; for anything non-draft use VOID.

tools.name: create_purchase_order
tools.description:
Create a Purchase Request or Purchase Order (pick via documentType). lineItem text field = `name`. Dates YYYY-MM-DD. `reference` auto-generates (timestamped) if omitted — must be unique per org.

PURCHASE_REQUEST: draft requisition by default (saveAsDraft true → DRAFT). Lifecycle DRAFT → (issue: saveAsDraft:false) → ACTIVE → (transition_purchase_order ACCEPT) → ACCEPTED. To order from a request, create it with saveAsDraft:false — a DRAFT request can be neither linked nor accepted.
PURCHASE_ORDER: draft by default; create with saveAsDraft:false to make it ACTIVE. Link to a request via purchaseRequestResourceId — the request MUST be ACTIVE or ACCEPTED (not DRAFT/VOID). Lifecycle DRAFT → ACTIVE → CONFIRMED (transition_purchase_order CONFIRM).

accountResourceId is required on each lineItem when not a draft. There is no order→bill link field — raise the bill separately with create_bill.

tools.name: get_purchase_order
tools.description: Get a Purchase Request or Purchase Order by resourceId — line items, totals, status, orderState (fulfillment), and purchaseRequestResourceId link.

tools.name: search_purchase_orders
tools.description: Search Purchase Requests or Purchase Orders (by documentType). Filter by status, contact, date, amount, currency, tag. Use the filter param for advanced/nested queries.

tools.name: update_purchase_order
tools.description: Update an editable Purchase Request or Purchase Order (reference, dates, line items, notes, custom fields). Line items are fully replaced — pass the complete array.

tools.name: transition_purchase_order
tools.description:
Advance or cancel a Purchase Request / Purchase Order lifecycle. action:
- ACCEPT — Purchase Request only (ACTIVE → ACCEPTED). The request must already be issued (saveAsDraft:false); a DRAFT can't be accepted. An ACTIVE request is already linkable, so accept is optional.
- CONFIRM — Purchase Order only (ACTIVE → CONFIRMED).
- VOID — either, when not a draft (cancels the document).
- DELETE — DRAFT only; for anything non-draft use VOID.

tools.name: convert_sale_order_to_invoice
tools.description: Convert a Sale Quote or Sale Order (pick via documentType) into an Invoice. Creates a NEW invoice — NON-IDEMPOTENT: each call makes another invoice. On a timeout or uncertain result, do NOT blind-retry — search invoices for one already linked to this order (saleOrderResourceId / saleQuoteResourceId) first. `reference` is required by the API; if omitted a unique placeholder is generated — pass your own to follow your org's numbering. `saveAsDraft` defaults false (the new invoice is ACTIVE). The source must not be VOID.

tools.name: convert_purchase_order_to_bill
tools.description: Convert a Purchase Request or Purchase Order (pick via documentType) into a Bill. Creates a NEW bill — NON-IDEMPOTENT: each call makes another bill. On a timeout or uncertain result, do NOT blind-retry — search bills for one already linked to this order (purchaseOrderResourceId / purchaseRequestResourceId) first. `reference` is required by the API; if omitted a unique placeholder is generated — pass your own to follow your org's numbering. `saveAsDraft` defaults false (the new bill is ACTIVE). The source must not be VOID.

tools.name: search_sale_order_line_items
tools.description: Search the LINE ITEMS across Sale Quotes or Sale Orders (line-item level, not document level). Filter by line text, parent order, contact, account, amount, open state, date. Use the filter param for advanced/nested queries.

tools.name: search_purchase_order_line_items
tools.description: Search the LINE ITEMS across Purchase Requests or Purchase Orders (line-item level, not document level). Filter by line text, parent order, contact, account, amount, open state, date. Use the filter param for advanced/nested queries.

tools.name: list_journals
tools.description: List journal entries. Paginated — response includes totalElements. Use limit/offset to page.

tools.name: get_journal
tools.description: Get full journal entry details by resourceId. Returns journal lines, amounts, status, and metadata.

tools.name: search_journals
tools.description: Search journals. Status uses ACTIVE (not FINALIZED). Supports comma-separated status/type.

tools.name: create_journal
tools.description:
Create a journal entry. IMPORTANT:
- Each entry needs accountResourceId, type (DEBIT or CREDIT), and amount.
- Total debits MUST equal total credits — the API rejects unbalanced journals.
- Search accounts first to get the correct resourceIds.
- Double-check your math: sum all DEBIT amounts and all CREDIT amounts — they must be equal.

Optional capsuleRecipe (JOURNAL_MANUAL base — ACCRUAL_REVERSAL, IFRS16_LEASE, LOAN_AMORTIZATION). DEFERRED_REVENUE/PREPAID_AMORTIZATION silently null here; pre-flight with preview_capsule_recipe. Mutex with capsuleResourceId. Rule 143.

tools.name: create_transfer_trial_balance
tools.description:
Create opening balance entries (Transfer Trial Balance). Used to set up initial account balances when migrating to a new accounting system.
- Always created as ACTIVE — no draft mode.
- Reference is auto-generated ("Transfer Trial Balance") — do NOT send a reference.
- Minimum 1 entry (unlike regular journals which require 2).
- Each entry needs accountResourceId, type (DEBIT or CREDIT), and amount (must be > 0).
- Skips lock date validation (opening balances are special).
- Uses journalEntries (NOT lines — this is a journal type).

tools.name: delete_journal
tools.description: Delete a draft journal entry.

tools.name: generate_trial_balance
tools.description: Generate a trial balance report.

tools.name: generate_balance_sheet
tools.description: Generate a balance sheet report.

tools.name: generate_profit_and_loss
tools.description: Generate a profit & loss (income statement) report.

tools.name: generate_cashflow
tools.description: Generate a cashflow report.

tools.name: generate_aged_ar
tools.description: Generate aged accounts receivable summary.

tools.name: generate_aged_ap
tools.description: Generate aged accounts payable summary.

tools.name: generate_cash_balance
tools.description: Generate a cash balance report showing cash position at a specific date. PREFERRED for cash position questions — use this (NOT balance sheet) when asked about total cash, cash on hand, available cash, or cash position across all bank accounts.

tools.name: generate_general_ledger
tools.description: Generate a general ledger report.

tools.name: list_bank_accounts
tools.description: List bank accounts connected to the organization.

tools.name: list_items
tools.description: List items (products & services). Returns internalName, itemCode, appliesToSale/Purchase. Paginated — response includes totalElements. Use limit/offset to page.

tools.name: search_items
tools.description: Search items. Use list_items to search by name (internalName is not a filter field).

tools.name: get_item
tools.description: Get full item details by resourceId.

tools.name: create_item
tools.description:
Create a new item. Auto-checks for duplicates by itemCode — returns existing item if found.
- saleItemName/purchaseItemName are auto-set from internalName if not provided.
- Set appliesToSale and/or appliesToPurchase to control where the item can be used.

tools.name: update_item
tools.description: Update an existing item. Only send fields you want to change — required fields are auto-merged from current state.

tools.name: delete_item
tools.description: Delete an item.

tools.name: bulk_upsert_items
tools.description: Max 500 items per call. Provide resourceId per item to update (partial — only changed fields needed, server preserves existing values). Omit resourceId to create (defaults: status=ACTIVE, itemCategory=NON_INVENTORY).

tools.name: list_tags
tools.description: List tags used for transaction categorization. Paginated — response includes totalElements. Use limit/offset to page.

tools.name: search_tags
tools.description: Search tags.

tools.name: create_tag
tools.description: Create a new tag. Automatically reuses existing tag if one with the same name exists.

tools.name: get_tag
tools.description: Get a tag by resourceId.

tools.name: update_tag
tools.description: Rename a tag.

tools.name: delete_tag
tools.description: Delete a tag.

tools.name: list_capsule_types
tools.description: List capsule types (e.g., PREPAID_EXPENSE, DEFERRED_REVENUE). Use to get capsuleTypeResourceId before creating capsules via create_capsule. NOT the same as list_capsule_recipes (which lists IFRS recipe descriptors with JSON Schema input contracts).

tools.name: list_capsules
tools.description: List capsules (transaction groupings for amortization, deferred revenue, etc.). Paginated — response includes totalElements. Use limit/offset to page.

tools.name: search_capsules
tools.description: Search capsules.

tools.name: get_capsule
tools.description: Get full capsule details by resourceId.

tools.name: create_capsule
tools.description: Create a new (legacy) capsule. List capsule types first to get the capsuleTypeResourceId. `title` is required and must be non-empty (blank/empty rejected with 422). For IFRS recipe-driven capsules (PREPAID_AMORTIZATION, LOAN_AMORTIZATION, etc.), use `preview_capsule_recipe` + `capsuleRecipe` payload on a trigger mutation instead — those create the capsule + scheduler atoms in one shot. This tool is for manually-grouped legacy capsules.

tools.name: update_capsule
tools.description: Update a capsule title or description. Required fields are auto-merged from current state.

tools.name: delete_capsule
tools.description: Delete a capsule.

tools.name: list_customer_credit_notes
tools.description: List customer credit notes (AR adjustments/refunds). Paginated — response includes totalElements. Use limit/offset to page.

tools.name: search_customer_credit_notes
tools.description: Search customer credit notes. Status: DRAFT, UNAPPLIED, PARTIALLY_APPLIED, APPLIED, VOID.

tools.name: get_customer_credit_note
tools.description: Get full customer credit note details including line items.

tools.name: create_customer_credit_note
tools.description:
Create a customer credit note. Saves as draft by default.
- Status when finalized is UNAPPLIED (not APPROVED).
- Line items use "name" for item description.
- reference MUST be unique — generate one with a timestamp if user doesn't specify.

tools.name: delete_customer_credit_note
tools.description: Delete a draft customer credit note.

tools.name: update_customer_credit_note
tools.description: Update a draft customer credit note (change amount, line items, contact, date, notes). Use when the user says "update", "change", "fix", or "correct" a credit note.

tools.name: finalize_customer_credit_note
tools.description: Finalize a draft customer credit note (set saveAsDraft=false). Status becomes UNAPPLIED.

tools.name: create_customer_credit_note_refund
tools.description: Record a refund payment against a customer credit note.

tools.name: list_customer_credit_note_refunds
tools.description: List refund payments for a customer credit note.

tools.name: list_supplier_credit_notes
tools.description: List supplier credit notes (AP adjustments/refunds). Paginated — response includes totalElements. Use limit/offset to page.

tools.name: search_supplier_credit_notes
tools.description: Search supplier credit notes. Status: DRAFT, UNAPPLIED, PARTIALLY_APPLIED, APPLIED, VOID.

tools.name: get_supplier_credit_note
tools.description: Get full supplier credit note details including line items.

tools.name: create_supplier_credit_note
tools.description:
Create a supplier credit note. Saves as draft by default.
- Status when finalized is UNAPPLIED (not APPROVED).
- Line items use "name" for item description.

tools.name: delete_supplier_credit_note
tools.description: Delete a draft supplier credit note.

tools.name: download_supplier_credit_note_pdf
tools.description: Download a supplier credit note as PDF. Returns { fileUrl } (may be pending/empty if not yet rendered).

tools.name: update_supplier_credit_note
tools.description: Update a draft supplier credit note (change amount, line items, contact, date, notes). Use when the user says "update", "change", "fix", or "correct" a credit note.

tools.name: finalize_supplier_credit_note
tools.description: Finalize a draft supplier credit note (set saveAsDraft=false). Status becomes UNAPPLIED.

tools.name: create_supplier_credit_note_refund
tools.description: Record a refund payment against a supplier credit note.

tools.name: list_supplier_credit_note_refunds
tools.description: List refund payments for a supplier credit note.

tools.name: list_currencies
tools.description: List currencies enabled for the organization. Returns code, name, symbol, isBaseCurrency.

tools.name: add_currency
tools.description: Enable one or more currencies for the organization.

tools.name: list_currency_rates
tools.description: List exchange rates for a specific currency. IMPORTANT: You MUST call list_currencies first to discover which currencies the org has enabled — never guess or assume currency codes.

tools.name: add_currency_rate
tools.description: Add or set an exchange rate for a currency. ALWAYS use this tool even when the user says "update rate" — it handles both new and existing rates. Rate is relative to the base currency. Call list_currencies first to get valid currency codes.

tools.name: update_currency_rate
tools.description: Update an EXISTING exchange rate record by its resourceId. Requires the rate resourceId from list_currency_rates. WARNING: If the user says "update the rate" or "set the rate", they almost always mean add_currency_rate (which creates/overwrites for a date). Only use this tool when explicitly modifying an existing rate record by ID.

tools.name: bulk_upsert_currency_rates
tools.description: Max 500 exchange rates per call. Auto-enables currencies not yet enabled in the org — no need to call add_currency first. Requires rateDirection per rate. Response surfaces per-row failures: `failedRows[]` (each with rowIndex, columnName, columnValue, errorCode, errorMessage) + `failedCount` alongside `resourceIds[]` for successful inserts. Omitting `rateApplicableTo` defaults it to `rateApplicableFrom - 0.999ms` (no temporal gap).

tools.name: list_tax_profiles
tools.description: List tax profiles (GST, VAT, etc.). Returns name, rate, tax type.

tools.name: list_tax_types
tools.description: List available tax types. Use the tax type code when creating tax profiles.

tools.name: create_tax_profile
tools.description: Create a new tax profile. List tax types first to get the taxTypeCode. Automatically checks for duplicates by name — returns existing profile if found.

tools.name: list_cash_in
tools.description: List cash-in entries (direct cash receipts). Paginated.

tools.name: create_cash_in
tools.description:
Record money received INTO a bank account from an EXTERNAL source (customer payment, refund received, deposit). For inter-account moves use create_cash_transfer.

accountResourceId MUST be a Bank/Cash account (from list_bank_accounts) — any other type fails. Cannot appear in lines (API enforces separation).

lines = offsetting entries: { accountResourceId, type (DEBIT/CREDIT), amount }. Offset accounts must be regular P&L or balance-sheet accounts (revenue, expense, asset, liability) — NOT bank/cash and NOT controlled accounts (AR/AP).

Optional capsuleRecipe (JOURNAL_DIRECT_CASH_IN base — LOAN_AMORTIZATION: cash-in = loan disbursement, recipe builds repayment schedule). Other recipes silently null here; pre-flight with preview_capsule_recipe. Mutex with capsuleResourceId. Rule 143.

tools.name: list_cash_out
tools.description: List cash-out entries (direct cash disbursements). Paginated.

tools.name: create_cash_out
tools.description:
Record money paid OUT FROM a bank account to an EXTERNAL party (expense, supplier payment, reimbursement, withdrawal). For inter-account moves use create_cash_transfer.

accountResourceId MUST be a Bank/Cash account (from list_bank_accounts). Cannot appear in lines (API enforces separation; error: "CashOut journal account cannot be in CashIn Entries").

lines = offsetting entries: { accountResourceId, type: 'DEBIT' (typical for the expense leg), amount > 0 }. Offset accounts must be regular P&L or balance-sheet accounts — NOT bank/cash and NOT controlled accounts (AR/AP). Example: { accountResourceId: "<expense-acct>", type: "DEBIT", amount: 100 }.

Optional capsuleRecipe — no v1 recipe lists JOURNAL_DIRECT_CASH_OUT; passing capsuleRecipe here silently nulls. Use create_cash_in or create_journal for LOAN_AMORTIZATION. Mutex with capsuleResourceId. Rule 143.

tools.name: get_cash_in
tools.description: Get a cash-in entry by resourceId.

tools.name: update_cash_in
tools.description: Update an existing cash-in entry (change amount, date, reference, notes, tags, or entry lines). Use when the user says "update", "change", "fix", or "adjust" a cash-in that was just created or found. Do NOT create a new entry — use this tool instead. NOTE: cash entries created without saveAsDraft:true are ACTIVE (finalized) and cannot be updated — they must be deleted and recreated. Optional capsuleRecipe retro-attaches a recipe; same base-type constraint as create variant. Mutex with capsuleResourceId. Rule 143.

tools.name: get_cash_out
tools.description: Get a cash-out entry by resourceId.

tools.name: update_cash_out
tools.description: Update an existing cash-out entry (change amount, date, reference, notes, tags, or entry lines). Use when the user says "update", "change", "fix", or "adjust" a cash-out that was just created or found. Do NOT create a new entry — use this tool instead. NOTE: cash entries created without saveAsDraft:true are ACTIVE (finalized) and cannot be updated — they must be deleted and recreated. Optional capsuleRecipe retro-attaches a recipe; same base-type constraint as create variant. Mutex with capsuleResourceId. Rule 143.

tools.name: list_cash_transfers
tools.description: List cash transfer entries. Paginated.

tools.name: create_cash_transfer
tools.description:
Move money between TWO OF YOUR OWN bank/cash accounts (petty-cash top-up, inter-account, FX between own accounts).

NOT for external parties — use create_cash_in (receiving) / create_cash_out (paying).

cashOut = source, cashIn = destination. Each side needs accountResourceId + amount. reference auto-generated if omitted. Do NOT send currency/exchangeRate (derived server-side).

tools.name: delete_cash_in
tools.description: Delete (void) a cash-in entry. Uses parentEntityResourceId from create response.

tools.name: delete_cash_out
tools.description: Delete (void) a cash-out entry. Uses parentEntityResourceId from create response.

tools.name: delete_cash_transfer
tools.description: Delete (void) a cash transfer. Uses parentEntityResourceId from create response.

tools.name: list_scheduled_invoices
tools.description: List scheduled (recurring) invoices. Paginated.

tools.name: list_scheduled_bills
tools.description: List scheduled (recurring) bills. Paginated.

tools.name: list_scheduled_journals
tools.description: List scheduled (recurring) journals. Paginated.

tools.name: get_bank_account
tools.description: Get full bank account details by resourceId.

tools.name: search_bank_records
tools.description: Search bank records for a specific account. Call list_bank_accounts first to get accountResourceId.

tools.name: search_cashflow_transactions
tools.description: Search cashflow transactions (unified ledger across cash entries / payments / journals). direction enum: PAYIN | PAYOUT.

tools.name: list_bookmarks
tools.description: List organization bookmarks (saved values/settings).

tools.name: get_bookmark
tools.description: Get a bookmark by resourceId.

tools.name: create_bookmarks
tools.description: Create one or more bookmarks. categoryCode must be one of: AUDIT_AND_ASSURANCE, BANKING_AND_FINANCE, BUDGETS_AND_CONTROLS, EMPLOYEES_AND_PAYROLL, EXTERNAL_DOCUMENTS, GENERAL_INFORMATION, OWNERS_AND_DIRECTORS, TAXATION_AND_COMPLIANCE, WORKFLOWS_AND_PROCESSES.

tools.name: update_bookmark
tools.description: Update an existing bookmark (name, value, or category). Use when modifying a previously created bookmark.

tools.name: list_org_users
tools.description: List organization users (team members). Returns name, email, roles.

tools.name: search_org_users
tools.description: Search organization users. For OR (name or email), use filter param.

tools.name: invite_org_user
tools.description:
Invite a new user to the organization.

userType drives access:
- ADMIN: full access to every module — moduleRoles is ignored.
- CUSTOM_USER: per-module access — supply moduleRoles for each module the user should reach.

Modules (8): ORGANIZATION, USER_MANAGEMENT, BILLING, ACCOUNTING, SALES, PURCHASES, REPORTS, FIXED_ASSET. Any module omitted for a CUSTOM_USER defaults to no access.

tools.name: update_org_user
tools.description:
Update an organization user's access. Provide userType to change their access tier, moduleRoles to change per-module access, or both.

moduleRoles behaves as a PARTIAL MERGE: modules you do not include keep their existing role on the user. To revoke access for a specific module, send it with roleCode NO_ACCESS — do NOT just omit it.

tools.name: remove_org_user
tools.description: Remove a user from the organization.

tools.name: list_payments
tools.description: List recent payments across all transaction types. Paginated.

tools.name: search_payments
tools.description: Search payments. direction enum: PAYIN | PAYOUT.

tools.name: download_export
tools.description:
Analytical / financial / audit report file (XLSX or PDF). Returns { fileName, fileUrl } (pre-signed, ~5 min). Use this tool, not export_records, for anomaly / audit / risk analyses, statements, aging, summaries, ledgers, statement-of-account.

Per-type date / scope (wrong set → 422 "X is required"):
- balance-sheet → snapshotDate
- cashflow, equity-movement → primaryStartDate + primaryEndDate (secondary* optional)
- bank-closing-balance → reportDate
- bank-reconciliation-* → startDate + endDate + bankAccountResourceId
- fixed-assets-reconciliation-summary → startDate + endDate + accountResourceId (fixed-asset UUIDs; search fixed_assets first)
- trial-balance, cash-balance, ar-report, ap-report → endDate
- other summaries (P&L, GL, sales/purchase, tax, fixed-assets-register, etc.) → startDate + endDate

For raw entity rows use export_records; for ad-hoc SELECT use preview_pseudo_sql / run_pseudo_sql_and_download. Cashflow returns the IAS 7 templated report (404 template_not_found if none configured, jaz-api Rule 147).

tools.name: preview_pseudo_sql
tools.description: Run a read-only pseudo-SQL SELECT against the curated reporting schema. SYNC — returns up to 100 rows + typed columns. For canonical reports (anomaly/audit/aging/P&L/BS/GL/statement-of-account) use download_export instead. DELETE/UPDATE/INSERT and multi-statement input are rejected (PSEUDOSQL_PARSE_ERROR). Query max 16384 chars (over → 422 validation_error). `truncated:true` means "more rows matched than were returned" — NOT "you hit the cap"; inspect rowCount vs preview cap or your LIMIT. Curated tables include invoices, bills, journals, payments, contacts, accounts and more — see jaz-pseudo-sql skill for full inventory + query patterns.

tools.name: export_pseudo_sql
tools.description: Kick off an async CSV export of a pseudo-SQL SELECT against the curated reporting schema. Returns { jobId, status }. Same DSL constraints as preview_pseudo_sql (SELECT-only, ≤16384 chars, single statement). Poll status via get_pseudo_sql_export — terminal states are COMPLETED (downloadUrl present, ~15min S3 expiry), FAILED, or EXPIRED. For one-shot "give me the CSV" use run_pseudo_sql_and_download instead. Supply idempotencyKey to dedupe across retries; same key + DIFFERENT query returns the prior job's result (server-side primary).

tools.name: get_pseudo_sql_export
tools.description: Poll a pseudo-SQL export job. Returns { jobId, status, rowCount?, truncated?, downloadUrl?, error? }. Terminal states: COMPLETED, FAILED, EXPIRED. `downloadUrl` is S3 pre-signed with ~15min expiry — fetch immediately. If a fetch returns 403 (expired), call this tool again for a fresh URL.

tools.name: run_pseudo_sql_and_download
tools.description: One-shot composite: kickoff async export → poll every 2s → return { jobId, status, downloadUrl, buffer? | localPath? }. Default 25s timeout (safe under typical 30s MCP provider timeout, configurable up to 90s). Idempotency-Key auto-set to sha256(query).slice(0,16) so same-query repeats dedupe. Default returns CSV buffer; pass downloadToFile=true to write to ~/Downloads/pseudo-sql-<jobId>.csv. On TIMED_OUT returns { status:'TIMED_OUT', jobId } — caller can retry via get_pseudo_sql_export. Use for "run this query and give me the CSV" flows; use export_pseudo_sql + get_pseudo_sql_export for fine-grained control.

tools.name: get_pseudo_sql_schema
tools.description: Live pseudo-SQL catalog (tables/columns/joins/functions) + agentSkillsDoc.content = canonical jaz-pseudo-sql.md (~30KB syntax guide). Call before any query. `version` is a stable cache key.

tools.name: plan_recipe
tools.description:
Offline. Plan an IFRS transaction (schedule + journals) WITHOUT posting. Returns RecipePlan: capsuleType, capsuleName, requiredAccounts, needsContact, steps (journal|bill|invoice|cash-in|cash-out), calculator output.

Supported (13): loan, lease, depreciation, prepaid-expense, deferred-revenue, fx-reval, ecl, provision, fixed-deposit, asset-disposal, accrued-expense, leave-accrual, dividend.

USE FOR: depreciation (sl/ddb/150db), amortization, ECL, lease (IFRS 16), hire purchase, loan, IAS 37 provisions, deferred revenue, fixed deposits, asset disposal, accrued expenses, leave-accrual, dividends. Never hand-construct.

ALTERNATIVE — server-side: 5 of these (loan/lease/prepaid-expense/deferred-revenue/accrued-expense) also available via capsuleRecipe payload on create_bill/create_invoice/create_journal/create_cash_in (creates capsule + scheduler atoms in one shot). Offline = plan, post manually; server-side = commit now.

fx-reval EXCEPTION: Jaz auto-handles period-end IAS 21.23 FX translation. fx-reval is VERIFICATION ONLY — do NOT execute_recipe(recipe: 'fx-reval'), it double-posts.

NEXT STEP: execute_recipe + startDate (offline) OR preview_capsule_recipe + capsuleRecipe payload on trigger mutation (server-side).

ALIASES (file→engine): prepaid-amortization→prepaid-expense, bank-loan→loan, ifrs16-lease→lease, bad-debt-provision→ecl, accrued-expenses→accrued-expense, fx-revaluation→fx-reval, declining-balance→depreciation. Pass the engine name.

tools.name: execute_recipe
tools.description:
Execute a recipe end-to-end — calculator + capsule + post all entries in one call. PREFERRED over manual creation (replaces ~20 tool calls). After plan_recipe, ALWAYS use this (not create_journal/create_invoice/create_cash_out).

Supported: loan, lease, depreciation, prepaid-expense, deferred-revenue, fx-reval, ecl, provision, fixed-deposit, asset-disposal, accrued-expense, leave-accrual, dividend.

ALTERNATIVE — for 5 recipes (loan, lease, prepaid-expense, deferred-revenue, accrued-expense) server-side capsule-recipe engine is also available via capsuleRecipe payload on create_bill/create_invoice/create_journal/create_cash_in.

Requires startDate. Auto-resolves accounts from CoA. Provide bankAccountName for recipes with cash-in/cash-out steps, contactName for recipes with invoice/bill steps.

tools.name: list_capsule_recipes
tools.description: Server-side recipe lifecycle (NOT the offline calculator — for offline planning use plan_recipe + execute_recipe). List all registered IFRS capsule recipes with per-version JSON Schemas. Source of truth for recipeName values used in capsuleRecipe payloads on trigger mutations (create_bill, create_journal, create_cash_in, etc.) — DON'T hard-code the 5 enum values. Each descriptor carries displayName, capsuleType, requiresBaseTransaction, allowedBaseTransactionTypes[], expectedAtomTypes[], and versions[] with inputSchema (JSON Schema Draft 7) per version.

tools.name: get_capsule_recipe
tools.description: Get one capsule recipe descriptor by enum name. inputSchema at `data.versions[0].inputSchema` (NOT top-level). Carries `allowedBaseTransactionTypes`, `x-accountClass` per *AccountResourceId slot, `x-baseTrxBinding` for auto-fills, and `versions[].templateSlots[]` — the slotKeys + supportedVariables you can pass to capsuleRecipe.templateOverrides (Customize Recipe). Use list_capsule_recipes for available names.

tools.name: preview_capsule_recipe
tools.description: Compute the blueprint a recipe would build, WITHOUT persisting. Returns { legs[], expectedOutput[], previewMarkdown }. **Pre-flight gate** before sending capsuleRecipe payloads — trigger mutations silently null on publish failure but preview surfaces every problem as a clean 422 (ERR_RECIPE_ACCOUNT_CURRENCY_MISMATCH, RECIPE_INVALID_BASE_TRANSACTION_TYPE, ERR_RECIPE_OVERRIDE_* — Rule 143). Also the gate for templateOverrides: invalid slotKeys/variables come back as ERR_RECIPE_OVERRIDE_* here. The base-trx triplet (baseTransactionResourceId, baseTransactionType, organizationResourceId) is all-or-nothing — supply all three to auto-fill totalAmount/currency/anchor dates, OR omit all three for a pure compute preview.

tools.name: resume_capsule_recipe
tools.description: Server-side recipe lifecycle. Resume a FAILED recipe job from the leg that failed. NOT idempotent — each call counts toward the 3-attempt limit. After 3 same-leg failures the job is terminally BLOCKED_AFTER_3_RESUME_ATTEMPTS and only rollback_capsule_recipe remains. Returns the updated CapsuleRecipeJob handle with status, resumeAttempts, terminalReason.

tools.name: rollback_capsule_recipe
tools.description: Server-side recipe lifecycle. Delete every scheduler atom posted by this capsule's recipe. dryRun=true returns the would-delete list without deleting (idempotent, safe to call repeatedly). dryRun=false performs the delete. Status enum: SUCCESS | BLOCKED | PARTIAL_ROLLBACK | DRY_RUN. PARTIAL_ROLLBACK with blockedAtomResourceIds[] means some atoms are referenced downstream — retry idempotent; escalate if persistent. Capsules NOT created via the recipe engine return 422 RECIPE_ROLLBACK_JOB_NOT_FOUND ("nothing to roll back").

tools.name: create_scheduled_journal
tools.description:
Recurring journal. Use `schedulerEntries` (NOT journalEntries) — each needs accountResourceId, amount, type (DEBIT|CREDIT), name. Use `repeat` (NOT frequency/interval): WEEKLY|MONTHLY|QUARTERLY|YEARLY. Debits must equal credits. Flat structure — reference, valueDate, saveAsDraft at top level.

Dynamic strings (reference/name/notes): {{Day}}, {{Date}}, {{Date+X}}, {{DateRange:X}}, {{Month}}, {{Month+X}}, {{MonthRange:X}}, {{Year}}, {{Year+X}}.

tools.name: create_scheduled_invoice
tools.description:
Recurring invoice. Use for fixed-amount recurrence where you want per-period control over currency / tax / line items. DIFFERENT from subscriptions: no proration, but currency/tax/items can differ per occurrence.

CRITICAL: saveAsDraft MUST be false (API rejects INVALID_SALE_STATUS). Use `repeat` (NOT frequency/interval): WEEKLY | MONTHLY | QUARTERLY | YEARLY. Each lineItem needs name, unitPrice, quantity, accountResourceId.

Dynamic strings (reference/name/notes): {{Day}}, {{Date}}, {{Date+X}}, {{DateRange:X}}, {{Month}}, {{Month+X}}, {{MonthRange:X}}, {{Year}}, {{Year+X}}.

tools.name: create_scheduled_bill
tools.description:
Create a scheduled (recurring) bill. CRITICAL:
- saveAsDraft MUST be false (API rejects INVALID_PURCHASE_STATUS otherwise).
- Use repeat (NOT frequency/interval): WEEKLY, MONTHLY, QUARTERLY, YEARLY.
- Each lineItem needs name, unitPrice, quantity, accountResourceId.
Dynamic strings (reference/name/notes): {{Day}}, {{Date}}, {{Date+X}}, {{DateRange:X}}, {{Month}}, {{Month+X}}, {{MonthRange:X}}, {{Year}}, {{Year+X}} — relative to transaction date.

tools.name: update_journal
tools.description: Update an existing journal entry. Can modify reference, valueDate, entries, notes. Set saveAsDraft=false to finalize. Optional capsuleRecipe retro-attaches a recipe; same base-type constraint as create variant. Mutex with capsuleResourceId. Rule 143.

tools.name: bulk_update_journals
tools.description:
Bulk-update journals — most common use is bulk-finalize (saveAsDraft: false on each row). Sync; per-row sequential execution; max 500 per call (~1-2 min wall-clock for a full 500).

bulk_finalize_drafts only handles invoices/bills/credit-notes — journals need this. Common monthly-close pattern: search_journals(filter:{capsuleResourceId:{eq:<id>}, status:{eq:'DRAFT'}}) → map to {resourceId, saveAsDraft: false} → bulk_update_journals.

Each row carries the same fields as update_journal (resourceId required + any of reference, valueDate, notes, tags, saveAsDraft). Omitted fields preserve existing.

Response: { updated: [{resourceId}], failed: [{resourceId, error, errorCode, status}], updatedCount, failedCount }. Per-row execution — partial results are normal; retry only failed[].resourceId. errorCode + status come from the underlying JazApiError when available.

tools.name: import_bank_statement
tools.description: Import a bank statement from URL or attachment ID. Processing is async — check magic workflows for status.

tools.name: add_bank_records
tools.description:
Create bank statement entries via JSON POST (1-100 records per call). For CSV/OFX file imports, use import_bank_statement instead.

Fields per record:
- amount (required): positive = cash-in, negative = cash-out
- transactionDate (required): YYYY-MM-DD
- description, payerOrPayee, reference: optional strings

Returns {data: {errors: []}} on success. accountResourceId must be a bank-type CoA account (find via list_bank_accounts).

tools.name: create_bt_from_attachment
tools.description: Create a business transaction (invoice/bill/credit note) from a file, a URL, or raw HTML (e.g. an email body) using AI extraction. Processing is async.

tools.name: get_magic_workflow_status
tools.description: Get the current status of specific magic workflow(s) by resourceId(s). Returns workflow details including status, document type, and results.

tools.name: get_attachments
tools.description: Get attachments for a business transaction (invoice, bill, journal, credit note, or order document).

tools.name: add_attachment
tools.description: Add an attachment to a business transaction. Provide sourceUrl (downloads and uploads as file) or attachmentId (links existing). Order documents (sale-quotes, sale-orders, purchase-requests, purchase-orders) support file/sourceUrl upload ONLY — attachmentId link-by-id is rejected for them.

tools.name: delete_attachment
tools.description: Delete an attachment from a business transaction (invoice, bill, journal, credit note, or order document).

tools.name: read_spreadsheet_rows
tools.description: Parse rows from a CSV/Excel URL. Returns rows as JSON objects keyed by header (max 500/call, paginatable via offset/limit). Excel response includes availableSheets[] — re-call with sheetIndex if the first sheet is Instructions. Use BEFORE bulk_upsert_* when the user attaches a tabular file.

tools.name: validate_invoice_draft
tools.description: Check if an invoice draft is ready to finalize. Returns missing fields, structured validation report, and ready status. Use before finalize_invoice to prevent errors.

tools.name: validate_bill_draft
tools.description: Check if a bill draft is ready to finalize. Returns missing fields, structured validation report, and ready status. Use before finalize_bill to prevent errors.

tools.name: validate_journal_draft
tools.description: Check if a journal draft is ready to finalize. Returns missing fields (accounts, amounts, date), structured validation, and ready status.

tools.name: validate_credit_note_draft
tools.description: Check if a credit note draft (customer or supplier) is ready to finalize. Returns missing fields, structured validation, and ready status.

tools.name: list_bank_rules
tools.description: List bank reconciliation rules (action shortcuts). Shows rule name, action type, and target bank account.

tools.name: get_bank_rule
tools.description: Get full bank rule details including configuration (allocation percentages, accounts, tax settings).

tools.name: search_bank_rules
tools.description: Search bank rules.

tools.name: create_bank_rule
tools.description:
Bank reconciliation rule — auto-matches bank records. Use directly on user request, don't just list existing.

Required: name, appliesToReconciliationAccount (bank account UUID, no "ResourceId" suffix), configuration.

configuration MUST nest under reconcileWithDirectCashEntry. Inside: reference (REQUIRED), amountAllocationType ("PERCENTAGE" | "FIXED" — server infers from arrays, don't send "FIXED_AND_PERCENTAGE"), percentageAllocation[] / fixedAllocation[].

Minimum: `{ reconcileWithDirectCashEntry: { amountAllocationType: "PERCENTAGE", reference: "{{bankReference}}", percentageAllocation: [{ organizationAccountResourceId, amount: 100 }] } }`

Optional inside configuration: contactResourceId, internalNotes, tags, currencySettings, taxCurrencySettings, taxInclusion, taxVatApplicable.

Dynamic strings (name + reference): {{bankReference}}, {{bankPayee}}, {{bankDescription}}.

tools.name: update_bank_rule
tools.description: Update an existing bank reconciliation rule. FULL REPLACEMENT — the API replaces the entire rule on update. You MUST provide the complete configuration, appliesToReconciliationAccount, and name every time. Use get_bank_rule first to read the current state, merge your changes, then call this tool with the full payload. Dynamic strings: {{bankReference}}, {{bankPayee}}, {{bankDescription}}.

tools.name: delete_bank_rule
tools.description: Delete a bank reconciliation rule.

tools.name: view_auto_reconciliation
tools.description: Generate auto-reconciliation suggestions for a bank account. READ-ONLY — does NOT write. Returns execution-ready `suggestions[]`: each carries `recommendedTool` (the commit tool to call), `execute` (ready-to-pass args — merge bankAccountResourceId for reconcile_magic_match), `confidenceTier` (high/medium/low), and `autoCommitEligible` (true ⇒ safe to commit without review). Drive the flow: for each suggestion, if `autoCommitEligible` call its `recommendedTool` with `execute`; otherwise surface for confirmation. Pass `includeRaw:true` to also get the unmapped payload. On high-volume accounts the engine can 500 (OOM) → the tool returns `{degraded:true}`; recover via the `clio jobs bank-recon match` cascade matcher.

tools.name: list_fixed_assets
tools.description: List fixed assets. Returns name, reference, type, category, status, purchase amount, net book value.

tools.name: get_fixed_asset
tools.description: Get full fixed asset details including depreciation schedule, accounts, disposal info.

tools.name: search_fixed_assets
tools.description: Search fixed assets.

tools.name: create_fixed_asset
tools.description:
Register a fixed asset linked to an existing purchase transaction (bill or journal).
- saveAsDraft defaults to true. Set to false to activate — requires ALL fields below.
- ACTIVE assets require: purchaseBusinessTransactionType + purchaseBusinessTransactionResourceId
  (links to the bill/journal that recorded the purchase).
- For standalone asset entry (no linked transaction), use transfer_fixed_asset instead.

tools.name: update_fixed_asset
tools.description: Update an existing fixed asset (draft or active). Can update name, depreciation settings, notes.

tools.name: delete_fixed_asset
tools.description: Delete a draft fixed asset. Cannot delete active/disposed assets.

tools.name: discard_fixed_asset
tools.description: Discard (write off) a fixed asset. Records the disposal and final depreciation.

tools.name: mark_fixed_asset_sold
tools.description: Mark a fixed asset as sold. Links to the sale transaction and records gain/loss. When the user asks to sell an asset, use this tool directly — retrieve the asset first with list_fixed_assets or search_fixed_assets to get the resourceId.

tools.name: transfer_fixed_asset
tools.description:
Register a previously purchased fixed asset with accumulated depreciation.
Use when the asset was acquired before using Jaz, imported from a legacy system, or purchased outside the system.
No linked purchase transaction needed — unlike create_fixed_asset which requires a bill/journal reference.
- bookValueAccumulatedDepreciationAmount: depreciation already incurred before registration
- All depreciation/account fields same as create_fixed_asset

tools.name: undo_fixed_asset_disposal
tools.description: Undo a fixed asset disposal (discard or sale). Restores the asset to active status. When the user asks to reverse or undo a disposal, use this tool directly.

tools.name: list_subscriptions
tools.description: List recurring subscriptions. Returns status, interval, next schedule date, amount, transaction type.

tools.name: get_subscription
tools.description: Get full subscription details including line items, schedule, and payment history.

tools.name: create_subscription
tools.description:
Subscription — auto-generates invoices on schedule WITH proration. DIFFERENT from scheduled_invoice: subscriptions prorate partial periods (credit notes for mid-period changes), but currency/tax/account are immutable after creation. Use for software licenses, retainers, recurring SaaS. Invoices only (no bills).

accountResourceId + taxProfileResourceId apply to ALL line items (immutable). interval: WEEKLY | MONTHLY | QUARTERLY | YEARLY. reference REQUIRED. proratedConfig (optional) tunes the first period; response carries bundledPeriodEndDate + bundledAmount when includeNextPeriod=true.

Dynamic strings (reference/lineItem name/notes): {{Day}}, {{Date}}, {{Date+X}}, {{DateRange:X}}, {{Month}}, {{Month+X}}, {{MonthRange:X}}, {{Year}}, {{Year+X}} — relative to transaction date.

tools.name: update_subscription
tools.description: Update an existing subscription (interval, end date, transaction template, or proratedConfig). When the user asks to update a subscription, use this tool directly. NOTE: accountResourceId and taxProfileResourceId on line items are immutable after creation — to change them, cancel and recreate. proratedConfig fields can be amended on update (proratedStartDate, proratedAdjustmentLineText, itemResourceId, includeNextPeriod).

tools.name: delete_subscription
tools.description: Delete a subscription. Removes the recurring schedule.

tools.name: cancel_subscription
tools.description: Cancel an active subscription. Prorates remaining period and stops future billing. Safe to call — subscriptions can be reactivated after cancellation. Always use this tool when the user asks to cancel a subscription. Must cancel before delete.

tools.name: search_scheduled_transactions
tools.description: Search scheduled transactions (subscriptions / recurring invoices / journals / cash entries).

tools.name: generate_vat_ledger
tools.description: Generate VAT/GST ledger report showing all tax transactions for the period.

tools.name: generate_equity_movement
tools.description: Generate equity movement report (statement of changes in equity).

tools.name: generate_bank_balance_summary
tools.description: Generate bank balance summary report — snapshot of all bank account balances at a point in time.

tools.name: generate_bank_recon_summary
tools.description: Generate bank reconciliation summary for a specific bank account. Essential for month-end close — call this for each in-scope bank account for the current entity and reporting period. Use for bank recon status, unreconciled items count, or reconciliation summaries.

tools.name: generate_bank_recon_details
tools.description: Generate detailed bank reconciliation report showing matched/unmatched items.

tools.name: generate_fa_summary
tools.description: Generate fixed assets summary report grouped by account, type, category, or status.

tools.name: generate_fa_recon_summary
tools.description: Generate fixed assets reconciliation summary — reconciles asset register with general ledger.

tools.name: generate_ar_report
tools.description: Generate accounts receivable aging report. Shows outstanding invoices grouped by aging buckets. Use this when discussing customer payment allocation, credit control, or AR status — generate the report to show the full picture.

tools.name: get_ledger_highlights
tools.description: Get ledger highlights — summary metadata about the org general ledger: transaction counts by type, date range, active accounts, active currencies, and cross-currency detection. No parameters needed.

tools.name: list_contact_groups
tools.description: List contact groups with their associated contacts. Used to segment customers/suppliers.

tools.name: get_contact_group
tools.description: Get contact group details including all associated contacts.

tools.name: search_contact_groups
tools.description: Search contact groups.

tools.name: create_contact_group
tools.description: Create a new contact group for segmenting contacts (e.g., "VIP Customers", "Government", "Overdue > 90 Days").

tools.name: update_contact_group
tools.description: Update a contact group name or members. NOTE: Update endpoint has a known 500 bug on some orgs — retry or recreate if it fails.

tools.name: delete_contact_group
tools.description: Delete a contact group. Does not delete the contacts themselves.

tools.name: list_custom_fields
tools.description: List custom fields defined for the organization. Custom fields add extra data to invoices, bills, credit notes, and payments.

tools.name: get_custom_field
tools.description: Get a custom field by resourceId. Returns full definition including appliesTo flags and list options.

tools.name: search_custom_fields
tools.description: Search custom fields.

tools.name: create_custom_field
tools.description:
Create a new custom field. Custom fields appear on transactions (invoices, bills, credit notes, payments).
- Use appliesTo to control which transaction types show this field.
- printOnDocuments controls whether the field appears on PDFs.

tools.name: update_custom_field
tools.description: Update a custom field definition. Can change name, description, printOnDocuments, and appliesTo flags.

tools.name: delete_custom_field
tools.description: Delete a custom field. Removes it from all future transactions.

tools.name: list_withholding_tax_codes
tools.description: List withholding tax codes (industry classification codes for PH/SG compliance). Returns full list (no pagination).

tools.name: search_tax_profiles
tools.description: Search tax profiles. Use appliesToSale / appliesToPurchase filters to scope by transaction type.

tools.name: get_tax_profile
tools.description: Get full tax profile details including tax rate, type code, and status.

tools.name: update_tax_profile
tools.description: Update an existing tax profile (name, rate, or status).

tools.name: list_inventory_items
tools.description: List inventory-tracked items with stock details, COGS accounts, and purchase/sale accounts.

tools.name: get_inventory_balance
tools.description: Get current inventory balance for a specific item — quantity on hand, average cost, base unit.

tools.name: create_inventory_item
tools.description: Create a new inventory-tracked item. Requires itemCode, name, costingMethod (FIXED or WAC), cogsResourceId, and account links. Use --input for full JSON body.

tools.name: delete_contact
tools.description: Delete a contact. Cannot delete contacts with existing transactions.

tools.name: delete_account
tools.description: Delete a chart of accounts entry. Cannot delete accounts with existing transactions.

tools.name: get_cash_transfer
tools.description: Get full cash transfer details by resourceId.

tools.name: download_credit_note_pdf
tools.description: Download a customer credit note as PDF. Returns a file URL.

tools.name: get_transaction_summary
tools.description:
Get a complete summary of any transaction — fetches the transaction, its attachments, and payment history in one call.
Works for invoices, bills, customer credit notes, supplier credit notes, and journals.

tools.name: bulk_finalize_drafts
tools.description: Finalize multiple draft transactions in one call. Attempts finalization for each item and returns per-item pass/fail results. Supports invoices, bills, and credit notes.

tools.name: bulk_invite_org_users
tools.description: Invite multiple users to the organization in one call. Accepts an array of user objects. Each user follows the same contract as invite_org_user. Returns per-user pass/fail results.

tools.name: bulk_create_contacts
tools.description:
Create multiple contacts in one call. Deduplicates against existing contacts (exact name match).
Returns per-contact result: created, skipped (duplicate), or failed.

tools.name: quick_fix_transactions
tools.description:
Bulk-update transactions of the same type. Only included fields change; omitted fields preserved.

Entities: invoices, bills, customer-credit-notes, supplier-credit-notes, journals, cash-entries, sale-schedules, purchase-schedules, subscription-schedules, journal-schedules.

Common attrs: valueDate, contactResourceId, tags, customFields, capsuleResourceId, currencySettings. Per-entity: invoices/bills + dueDate; cash-entries use currencySetting SINGULAR ({rateFunctionalToSource, exchangeToken}); journal-schedules + startDate/endDate/interval. Full matrix in jaz-api Rule 107.

Tags: string[] max 50 items × 50 chars.

Response: { updated, failed: [{resourceId, error, errorCode}] }. HTTP 207 = partial — retry only failed resourceIds.

tools.name: quick_fix_line_items
tools.description:
Bulk-update line items across transactions. Only included fields change.

Request patterns:
- ARAP + accounting (invoices, bills, CNs, journals, cash-entries): lineItemResourceIds + attributes.
- Sale/purchase/subscription schedules: schedulerUpdates with arrayIndex (zero-based).
- Journal schedules: schedulerUpdates with lineItemResourceId (UUID), NOT arrayIndex.

Common line attrs: name, quantity, unit, unitPrice, discount, organizationAccountResourceId, taxProfileResourceId, classifierConfig. Purchase + withholdingTax. Journals/cash-entries use amount + description instead of unit/unitPrice. Full matrix in jaz-api Rule 107.

Response: { updated, failed: [{resourceId, error, errorCode}] }. HTTP 207 = partial.

tools.name: list_nano_classifiers
tools.description: List nano classifiers (tracking categories). Paginated. Nano classifiers tag line items with structured categories.

tools.name: get_nano_classifier
tools.description: Get a nano classifier by resourceId. Returns type and all classes.

tools.name: search_nano_classifiers
tools.description: Search nano classifiers (tracking categories).

tools.name: create_nano_classifier
tools.description: Create a nano classifier (tracking category). Provide a type name and list of class names. printable defaults to true (NOTE: printable: false is currently rejected by a server bug — always use true).

tools.name: update_nano_classifier
tools.description: Update a nano classifier — change type name, modify classes, or toggle printable.

tools.name: delete_nano_classifier
tools.description: Delete a nano classifier.

tools.name: list_invoice_payments
tools.description: List all payments recorded against an invoice. Returns payment records with amounts, dates, and methods.

tools.name: list_invoice_credits
tools.description: List all credit notes applied to an invoice.

tools.name: reverse_invoice_credit
tools.description: Reverse (unapply) a credit note from an invoice. The credit note becomes UNAPPLIED again.

tools.name: list_bill_payments
tools.description: List all payments recorded against a bill. Returns payment records with amounts, dates, and methods.

tools.name: list_bill_credits
tools.description: List all credit notes applied to a bill.

tools.name: reverse_bill_credit
tools.description: Reverse (unapply) a supplier credit note from a bill. The credit note becomes UNAPPLIED again.

tools.name: get_scheduled_invoice
tools.description: Get a scheduled (recurring) invoice by resourceId.

tools.name: update_scheduled_invoice
tools.description: Update a scheduled invoice — change schedule settings and/or the invoice template.

tools.name: delete_scheduled_invoice
tools.description: Delete a scheduled (recurring) invoice.

tools.name: get_scheduled_bill
tools.description: Get a scheduled (recurring) bill by resourceId.

tools.name: update_scheduled_bill
tools.description: Update a scheduled bill — change schedule settings and/or the bill template.

tools.name: delete_scheduled_bill
tools.description: Delete a scheduled (recurring) bill.

tools.name: get_scheduled_journal
tools.description: Get a scheduled (recurring) journal by resourceId.

tools.name: update_scheduled_journal
tools.description: Update a scheduled journal — change schedule settings and/or the journal template.

tools.name: delete_scheduled_journal
tools.description: Delete a scheduled (recurring) journal.

tools.name: get_payment
tools.description: Get a specific payment record by resourceId. Returns payment amount, method, date, and reference.

tools.name: update_payment
tools.description: Update a payment record — correct amount, reference, date, method, or account.

tools.name: delete_payment
tools.description: Delete (void) a payment record. The associated invoice/bill balance is restored.

tools.name: get_export_columns
tools.description: List all exportable columns for an entity type, including their paths and data types. Use paths from this response to build custom column sets for export_records or preview_export_records.

tools.name: preview_export_records
tools.description: Preview an export before generating the file. Returns totalRecords, up to 10 sample rows, resolved column definitions, and a human-readable filterDescription. Use this to confirm scope before calling export_records. Pass either query OR filter, never both.

tools.name: export_records
tools.description:
Raw entity-row XLSX export for one entity type (invoices/bills/contacts/items/journals/bank records/cashflow/fixed assets — one row per record, with column selection). Returns pre-signed download URL (~5 min), fileName, totalCount. Pass query OR filter, never both. Use preview_export_records first to confirm scope.

DO NOT use this for analytical / audit reports, financial statements, aging, summary reports, or statement-of-account — those go through download_export.

tools.name: search_background_jobs
tools.description: Search and poll background jobs. ANY operation that returns a jobId (bulk_upsert_contacts, bulk_upsert_items, bank statement import, magic file processing, etc.) can be tracked here. Filter by resourceId (the jobId value) to poll a specific job. Poll until status is SUCCESS, FAILED, or PARTIAL_SUCCESS. Use processedCount/failedCount/totalRecords for progress. IMPORTANT: filter by resourceId field, NOT jobId — they differ.

tools.name: bulk_upsert_contacts
tools.description:
Bulk create/update contacts (max 500). resourceId per row → update (partial; omitted fields preserve existing). Omit resourceId → create. Async; returns jobId → poll search_background_jobs by resourceId; PARTIAL_SUCCESS data[0].errorDetails. Per-row failures in errorDetails (NOT failedRows — that's the sync pattern). On PARTIAL_SUCCESS: succeeded rows are committed; re-submit only failed rowIndex entries.

Whole-batch 422 (validate before submit): (1) each contact must have at least one of customer:true OR supplier:true (after defaults + backfill); (2) emailList unique within a contact (case-insensitive after trim); (3) paymentTerms.value must be positive integer when paymentTerms.name != "CUSTOM"; (4) no duplicate names within batch (after whitespace+case normalize); (5) billingAddress / shippingAddress: addressLine1 required when address object provided.

tools.name: bulk_upsert_invoices
tools.description:
Max 500 invoices per call. FLAT shape — ONE line per invoice via itemDescription + totalAmount + invoiceAccountResourceId at row level. For multi-line, use bulk_upsert_invoice_line_items (nested lineItems[]).

Natural key: invoiceReference (rows sharing one within a batch are MERGED — last wins). resourceId per row → update by UUID; omit → upsert by invoiceReference.

ASYNC: returns jobId → poll search_background_jobs(filter:{resourceId:{eq:jobId}}); PARTIAL_SUCCESS → data[0].errorDetails. Dates ISO 8601 (YYYY-MM-DD); dateFormat field removed.

tools.name: bulk_upsert_invoice_line_items
tools.description: Nested line items per invoice (max 500 invoices per call). Each row carries lineItems[] under its parent invoice (scoped by invoiceReference). Async; returns jobId → poll search_background_jobs by resourceId; PARTIAL_SUCCESS data[0].errorDetails. Dates are ISO 8601 only.

tools.name: bulk_upsert_bills
tools.description: Max 500 bills per call. FLAT shape: ONE line per bill via `itemDescription` + `totalAmount` + `billAccountResourceId` at row level. For multi-line bills use `bulk_upsert_bill_line_items` instead — that variant takes nested `lineItems[]`. Natural key: `billReference` (rows sharing one within a batch are MERGED — last wins). Async; returns jobId → poll search_background_jobs by resourceId; PARTIAL_SUCCESS data[0].errorDetails. Dates are ISO 8601 only — dateFormat field was removed.

tools.name: bulk_upsert_bill_line_items
tools.description: Nested line items per bill (max 500 bills per call). Each row carries lineItems[] under its parent bill (scoped by billReference). Async; returns jobId → poll search_background_jobs by resourceId; PARTIAL_SUCCESS data[0].errorDetails.

tools.name: bulk_upsert_customer_credit_notes
tools.description: Max 500 customer credit notes per call. Natural key: creditNoteReference. Async; returns jobId → poll search_background_jobs by resourceId; PARTIAL_SUCCESS data[0].errorDetails. Dates are ISO 8601 only.

tools.name: bulk_upsert_supplier_credit_notes
tools.description: Max 500 supplier credit notes per call. Natural key: creditNoteReference. Async; returns jobId → poll search_background_jobs by resourceId; PARTIAL_SUCCESS data[0].errorDetails. Dates are ISO 8601 only.

tools.name: bulk_upsert_journals
tools.description: Max 500 manual journals per call. NATURAL KEY: `journalReference` (NOT `reference` — every other bulk-upsert uses entityReference, this one is asymmetric). LEGS field: `journalEntries[]` (NOT `entries[]` — different from `clio journals create` which uses entries). Each leg has `accountResourceId` + `debitAmount` + `creditAmount` (debit + credit must balance per row). Async; returns jobId → poll search_background_jobs by resourceId; PARTIAL_SUCCESS data[0].errorDetails. Dates are ISO 8601 (YYYY-MM-DD) only — dateFormat field was removed.

tools.name: bulk_upsert_fixed_assets
tools.description:
Bulk create/update fixed assets (max 500). Natural key: reference.

⚠️ DATE FIELD MISMATCH: REQUEST uses valueDate (NOT purchaseDate); GET response uses purchaseDate. Sending purchaseDate → cryptic 400 "Invalid request body".

Required per row: reference, registrationType (NEW | TRANSFER). Recommended: typeCode, typeName, category (TANGIBLE | INTANGIBLE), cost (synonym: purchaseAmount), valueDate, depreciationStartDate, effectiveLife (synonym: usefulLifeMonths), depreciationMethod, purchaseAssetAccountResourceId, depreciationExpenseAccountResourceId, accumulatedDepreciationAccountResourceId. Account fields = UUIDv4.

ASYNC: returns jobId → poll search_background_jobs(filter:{resourceId:{eq:jobId}}); on PARTIAL_SUCCESS read data[0].errorDetails. Dates ISO 8601 (YYYY-MM-DD).

tools.name: quick_reconcile
tools.description: Bulk-reconcile bank statement entries against a list of journals (max 500). Reconciliation mode is hardcoded to QUICK_RECON. Async; returns jobId → poll search_background_jobs by resourceId; PARTIAL_SUCCESS data[0].errorDetails. Caller provides bankAccountResourceId + journalsForReconciliation[] (each row: bankStatementEntryResourceId, journalReference, journalAccountResourceId).

tools.name: apply_bank_rule
tools.description: Apply a pre-configured bank rule (action shortcut) to a batch of bank statement entries (max 500). Async — returns a jobId. Poll search_background_jobs filtered by resourceId. The rule executes its configured action (e.g. RECONCILE_WITH_DIRECT_CASH_ENTRY) on each entry.

tools.name: reconcile_direct_cash_entry
tools.description: Reconcile a bank statement entry with a single cash-in / cash-out line. Direction (cash-in vs cash-out) is INFERRED from the bank entry sign. Sync — returns the reconciled entry status. NOT idempotent: calling twice on the same bankStatementEntryResourceId creates duplicate journals. Confirm reconciled-state via view_auto_reconciliation before retrying.

tools.name: reconcile_cash_journal
tools.description: Reconcile a bank statement entry with a multi-line cashflow journal (multiple cash splits, max 200 lines). Sync — returns the reconciled entry status. NOT idempotent — confirm reconciled-state before retrying.

tools.name: reconcile_manual_journal
tools.description: ⚠️ CALLER PROVIDES ONLY THE OFFSET LEG(S) — backend AUTO-ADDS the bank-side leg from the statement entry. Sending both debit AND credit legs → 422 "sum of debit and credit amounts are not equal" (your sides duplicate after the auto-add). Reconcile a bank statement entry with a double-entry manual journal. Sync — returns the reconciled entry status. valueDate prefills from bank entry if omitted. NOT idempotent.

tools.name: reconcile_cash_transfer
tools.description: Reconcile a bank statement entry with an inter-account transfer. Sync — returns the reconciled entry status. amount is required only when the counterparty account is in a non-functional currency. NOT idempotent.

tools.name: reconcile_invoice_receipt
tools.description: CREATES A NEW invoice and auto-reconciles it to a bank statement entry (AR). To match an EXISTING open invoice instead, use reconcile_with_payments (do not create a duplicate). ⚠️ BSE must have `paymentDirection: PAYIN` (money in — AR). Produce via `bank add-records` with a POSITIVE amount (creates `credit_amount > 0` → PAYIN). Statement-imported BSEs from `bank import` also work. The error code "Invalid business transaction type" (422) is misleadingly named — the actual gate is direction, not entry type. Sync — returns the reconciled entry status. valueDate / dueDate / payment amount prefill from the bank entry if omitted. NOT idempotent.

tools.name: reconcile_bill_receipt
tools.description: CREATES A NEW bill and auto-reconciles it to a bank statement entry (AP). To match an EXISTING open bill instead, use reconcile_with_payments (do not create a duplicate). ⚠️ BSE must have `paymentDirection: PAYOUT` (money out — AP). Produce via `bank add-records` with a NEGATIVE amount (creates `debit_amount > 0` → PAYOUT). Statement-imported BSEs from `bank import` also work. The error code "Invalid business transaction type" (422) is misleadingly named — the actual gate is direction, not entry type. Sync — returns the reconciled entry status. valueDate / dueDate / payment amount prefill from the bank entry if omitted. NOT idempotent.

tools.name: reconcile_with_payments
tools.description: USE THIS to match a bank statement entry to an EXISTING open bill / invoice / payment (NOT reconcile_bill_receipt or reconcile_invoice_receipt, which CREATE a new bill/invoice). Pass the open transaction's cashflowTransactionResourceId (from search_cashflow_transactions or a view_auto_reconciliation suggestion) in businessTransactionPayments[] — this CREATES the payment AND reconciles in one call; no pay_bill / pay_invoice first. FX is resolved server-side — pass NO rate for the common case (only supply paymentAmount + currencySettings for the rare bill-currency ≠ bank-currency case). Sync — returns the reconciled entry status. NOT idempotent: a blind retry double-creates a payment; confirm reconciled-state via search_bank_records(status: RECONCILED) before retrying.

tools.name: reconcile_magic_match
tools.description: Bulk-accept MAGIC_MATCH suggestions: reconcile many bank entries against EXISTING open business transactions and/or payments in one call (max 500 entries). Feed entries from view_auto_reconciliation suggestions. Returns a per-entry split { reconciled[], failed[] } — a 200 with a non-empty failed[] is a PARTIAL success; surface failed entries (with their errorCode) and loop. Entry-level idempotency-keyed server-side. Use reconcile_with_payments for a single precise match; use this to accept a batch of suggestions at once.

tools.name: reconcile_learned_prediction
tools.description: Accept an ML learned-prediction suggestion to reconcile a bank entry. predictedPayload + predictedPayloadSchemaVersion are passed through VERBATIM from a view_auto_reconciliation suggestion (recommendationType MAGIC_RECONCILE_WITH_CASH_IN_OUT) — do not construct them by hand. Pass retryToken to force a fresh journal on an edit-then-retry; omit it for an idempotent replay. Sync — returns the reconciled entry status. On failure (stale/invalid payload), fall back to reconcile_with_payments — do NOT retry the same opaque payload.

tools.name: validate_drafts
tools.description:
BULK validate up to 500 draft business transactions in ONE call, mixing any btType. SYNC — returns per-item errors + display data; no state change. Run before convert_drafts_to_active.

btType enum: SALE = invoice, PURCHASE = bill, SALE_CREDIT_NOTE = customer CN, PURCHASE_CREDIT_NOTE = supplier CN. Journals NOT in this enum.

Each result carries contactSignals (Mid-7: cadence, outliers, severity, divergences from contact's modal pattern; null if no contact / no history) and breakdown (Balance-panel payload: line items + subtotal/tax/total/balance/exchange rate). Top-level contactSignalsMeta.unavailable=true → freshness layer offline for whole batch.

USE THIS to score a specific draft against contact history. For stand-alone "what does this contact normally look like?" without a draft → use get_contact_signals.

tools.name: convert_drafts_to_active
tools.description: BULK promote — finalize up to 500 draft business transactions to ACTIVE in ONE call, mixing any combination of invoices, bills, and credit notes (no per-type tools needed). Async; returns jobId → poll search_background_jobs by resourceId; PARTIAL_SUCCESS data[0].errorDetails. NOT idempotent: a second call on already-promoted drafts returns 422. Filter the draft list by status: DRAFT before submitting. Pair with validate_drafts for a pre-flight check on large batches.

tools.name: submit_drafts_for_approval
tools.description: BULK submit — route up to 500 draft business transactions into the approval workflow in ONE call, mixing any combination of invoices, bills, and credit notes. Async; returns jobId → poll search_background_jobs by resourceId; PARTIAL_SUCCESS data[0].errorDetails. NOT idempotent on drafts that already have an in-flight approval request.

tools.name: search_help_center
tools.description: Search the Jaz help center for how-to articles, feature guides, and accounting concepts. Use this for "how do I..." or "what is..." questions about Jaz: bank reconciliation, GST/VAT filing, multi-currency, fixed assets, custom fields, integrations, and so on. Returns top matching articles with title, section, content, and source URL.
